# Baton

> Runtime-agnostic multi-agent orchestrator. Single Go binary. Delegates coding tasks to external agent runtimes with structured context, deterministic phase execution, and self-healing feedback loops.

## What Baton Does

Baton orchestrates multiple AI coding agents (Claude Code, OpenCode, aider, etc.) from a single CLI. It dispatches structured task specs to workers, tracks their output, enforces role boundaries, and manages retry/escalation loops — all without calling LLMs directly.

Two execution modes:
- **Simple**: One task, one worker, one result (`baton run`)
- **Pipeline**: 16-phase deterministic orchestration with roles, retries, loop detection, and self-annealing (`baton pipeline run`)

## Core Concepts

- **Task Spec**: YAML file defining what, why, constraints, context files, acceptance criteria
- **Runtime**: External agent CLI (claude, opencode, aider) configured in `agents.yaml`
- **Phase Machine**: 16 sequential phases (setup → implementation → review → test → completion)
- **Roles**: Lead (plan), Developer (code), Reviewer (review), Test Lead (plan tests), Tester (write tests)
- **BATON Protocol**: Stdout markers (BATON:H heartbeat, BATON:P progress, BATON:C completion, BATON:S stuck, BATON:N notes)
- **Scratchpad**: Per-task memory across retries to prevent repeating failed approaches
- **L1/L2 Loops**: L1 = retry within phase, L2 = loop back from verification to implementation
- **Self-Annealing**: Event log mining → pattern detection → config patch suggestions

## Installation

```bash
go install github.com/yosephbernandus/baton@latest
```

## Quick Start

```bash
# Setup
mkdir -p .baton/specs
# Create .baton/agents.yaml with runtime configs
# Create .baton/project-brief.md with project context

# Simple mode
baton run --spec .baton/specs/task.yaml --task-id t1
baton result t1 --output

# Pipeline mode
baton pipeline run .baton/specs/feature.yaml --complexity MEDIUM
```

## CLI Commands

### Dispatch & Monitor
- `baton run --spec <path> --task-id <id>` — dispatch task to worker
- `baton pipeline run <spec.yaml> [--complexity LEVEL]` — run 16-phase pipeline
- `baton status` — list all tasks
- `baton monitor` — live TUI dashboard

### Results & Feedback
- `baton result <id> --output` — read worker output
- `baton wait <id> [id...]` — block until tasks complete
- `baton respond <id> --answer "..." --resume` — answer clarification
- `baton escalate <id> --reason "..."` — escalate to human
- `baton advise <id>` — view advisor context for stuck task

### Session & Analysis
- `baton session status` — pipeline session state
- `baton session reset` — clear session for fresh start
- `baton feedback` — analyze event log for patterns
- `baton anneal generate` — generate config patches from patterns

### Control
- `baton kill <id>` — kill running task
- `baton cost` — cost tracking summary

## Project Structure

```
.baton/
  agents.yaml          — runtime and pipeline config
  project-brief.md     — prepended to all worker prompts
  specs/               — task spec YAML files
  skills/              — domain context directories (backend/, frontend/, etc.)
  tasks/               — runtime state per task
  events.ndjson        — append-only event log
  session.yaml         — pipeline crash recovery manifest
```

## Configuration

See `INTEGRATION.md` for full setup guide including:
- Runtime configuration (Claude Code, OpenCode, aider)
- Pipeline phase machine settings
- Role-specific model routing
- Domain skills setup
- Escalation advisor (opt-in)
- Self-annealing feedback config

## Key Files

- `INTEGRATION.md` — step-by-step integration guide
- `ARCHITECTURE.md` — full system design
- `CLAUDE.md` — conventions and build commands
- `docs/adr/` — architecture decision records (ADR 001-018)
- `docs/conversational-worker-protocol.md` — IPC protocol details
